name: Graphs
description: Visual way to show relationships between two quantities

content.overview: A graph is just a picture showing how one thing changes with another. Instead of numbers in a table, you draw it. Way easier to see what's happening! {{viz:diagram-01}}
content.explanation:
Graphs let you SEE relationships instead of just reading numbers. {{viz:chart-01}}

Basic setup: two axes (usually x and y). Each point on the graph shows a pair of values. Like: at time x=5 seconds, position y=20 meters.

**Why graphs rock:**
- You can see patterns instantly
- Straight line = simple relationship
- Curve = something more complicated
- Steep = changes fast
- Flat = changes slowly

**Graph types in physics:**
- **Line graphs:** distance vs time, temperature vs time, etc.
- **Scatter plots:** data points that might not be perfectly connected
- **Bar graphs:** comparing quantities
- **Curved graphs:** when relationships get complicated {{viz:anim-01}}

Graphs are a TOOL. They make data talk to you instead of hiding in numbers.
keyPoints: Steepness shows how fast things change
content.whyItMatters: Graphs are how scientists communicate. You'll see graphs in every physics textbook, lab report, and research paper. Understanding them is essential.
